passions_detected.false_judgement: That being seen as knowledgeable/witty is necessary for respect
That not following through on commitments means irredeemable failure

causal_observations.description: Over-planning and over-preparing as avoidance — 'overusing the preparation stage of a goal which drains the energy/motivation from taking the first step'
Self-blame as false assent — 'Blame is a way of explaining things that require more consideration and effort to properly determine root cause'

value_observations.observed_as: lists specific egoistic needs to release: fitting in, shocking, being contradictory, impressing, intellectual humour
detailed list of biases to surrender: my way, know-it-all, scoffing, past experience always relevant

virtue_observations.evidence: Deep engagement with wisdom vs its appearance. Pass/fail rules for speech formulation. 'You can never cross the same river twice' applied to staying present.
Noticed connection between disciplined mind and mood — 'Journalling has used up some inquisitive self examination in a meaningful way which has meant less often intrusive thoughts'
'Talking to myself about something to get the feel-good hit despite not having actually doing the action yet' — recognises courage gap between talk and action

key_passages.summary: Speech discipline framework — pass/fail rules: can emotion be separated? What's the motivation? If by a virtue then express it, if by fantasy of outcome then don't.
Self-forgiveness: blamed himself for not reaching out, for ignorance, for dishonour, for not seeing the obvious. 'I forgive myself for taking the less painful, less real opinion in the past.'
Progress evidence: 'Don't feel the same shame. Outlook is more positive as I have found a fulfilment & patience. Outlook is can-do.'
